THOUGHT:
Currently, the bibliography only contains Sculley et al. (2015), which addresses systemic ML engineering challenges and technical debt. To comprehensively cover the workshop's focus on real-world deep learning pitfalls, I urgently need to cite foundational work on distribution shift and the generalization gap between controlled benchmarks and production environments. This next citation will simultaneously fulfill multiple required categories: highlighting research gaps (offline vs. in-the-wild performance), providing a dataset/benchmark citation, comparing empirical findings across domains, and supporting arguments about why models fail under real-world conditions. A seminal benchmark paper on in-the-wild distribution shifts is the most critical missing piece to ground the technical discussion of real-world failures.
